key_components: Per-operation error handling with aggregate failure tracking

patterns_discovered.description: Uses database outbox for tracking pending sync operations, enabling reliable distributed sync
Leverages WorkManager with coroutines for background task execution with network constraints
Tracks individual operation failures but continues processing remaining operations, returning partial success when some operations succeed
Operations transition through states: pending → SYNCING → deleted (success) or FAILED
Failure count aggregation determines final Result (success, partial success retry)

gotchas_discovered.description: Actual HTTP requests to sync data are not yet implemented; only structure exists with comments indicating intended implementation
No token/credential handling for server requests; will need to be added during actual API implementation
Uses e.printStackTrace() instead of production-grade logging framework; mentions Timber as TODO
No exponential backoff or sophisticated retry logic; retries entire batch vs individual operations
Database operations lack explicit timeout configuration; potential for long-running queries
Does not distinguish between network errors (retry-worthy) vs business logic errors (permanent)

approach_outcome.summary: SyncWorker infrastructure successfully created with proper CoroutineWorker integration and outbox pattern implementation
approach_outcome.completeness: Framework complete; actual server sync integration deferred to future subtasks
approach_outcome.quality: Well-structured with comprehensive error handling and documentation; ready for API integration

recommendations.details: Replace TODO with actual HTTP calls using operation type dispatcher (CREATE_ITEM, UPDATE_ITEM, DELETE_ITEM)
Implement token refresh, credential injection, and secure header management for API requests
Integrate Timber or similar framework for production-grade logging with context
Implement exponential backoff, operation-level retry counts, and network error detection

recommendations.item: Add comprehensive unit and integration tests
recommendations.details: Test success case, partial failure case, all-failure case, and database state transitions
Track retry attempts per operation to avoid infinite retries of problematic operations
Set explicit timeouts for database queries and consider adding per-operation timeout thresholds
